title: Economic Bulletin Issue 8, 2025
summary: The ECB Governing Council kept its three key interest rates unchanged at its December 18, 2025 meeting, with updated projections showing headline inflation expected at 2.1% (2025), 1.9% (2026), 1.8% (2027), and 2.0% (2028), while GDP growth is projected at 1.4% (2025), 1.2% (2026), 1.4% (2027), and 1.4% (2028). The Council emphasized a data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting approach without pre-committing to a specific rate path.
key_points: ECB kept three key rates unchanged at December 18, 2025 Governing Council meeting
Headline HICP inflation projections: 2.1% (2025), 1.9% (2026), 1.8% (2027), 2.0% (2028) — revised up 0.2pp for 2026 vs September projections
Core inflation (excluding energy and food) projections: 2.4% (2025), 2.2% (2026), 1.9% (2027), 2.0% (2028)
Real GDP growth: 1.4% (2025), 1.2% (2026), 1.4% (2027), 1.4% (2028) — revised up over the full projection horizon
Current euro area headline inflation stood at 2.1% in November 2025; unemployment at 6.4% (October 2025)
Services inflation rose to 3.5% in November 2025, while wage growth is expected to moderate toward 3% by late 2026
why_it_matters: This ECB Economic Bulletin provides the official macroeconomic outlook that will guide ECB policy decisions. The upward revision to 2026 inflation projections (due to services and wage dynamics) suggests the ECB may face sustained pressure on its easing path. The document signals no pre-commitment to rate cuts, making upcoming data releases critical for market participants positioning around ECB policy.
macro_context: The report shows a relatively benign medium-term inflation outlook converging to 2%, supported by wage moderation and euro appreciation. However, near-term services inflation (3.5% in November) remains above target. Growth projections were revised upward reflecting resilient domestic demand and reduced trade policy uncertainty. The ECB emphasizes data-dependency, suggesting policy flexibility but no urgency to cut rates further.
notes: Source is highly relevant to macro catalysts as it provides official ECB macroeconomic projections and policy stance. Not relevant to market structure. Published December 2025, approximately 3 months before TODAY (2026-03-31), making this a recent source. Truncation at end of risk assessment section is a minor limitation; core projections and policy guidance are complete.
